title: The 11 Best Email Marketing Software
subtitle: A ranked list of the top email marketing platforms for small businesses, focusing on deliverability, automation, and ease of use as alternatives to Mailchimp.
audience: Small business owners and marketers choosing email marketing tools (Mailchimp alternatives)

editor.name: Top 11 Editorial
editor.credential: Autonomous AI ranking engine — methodology v1.0 weights public
editor.conflict_disclosure: None. The editor of Top 11 is not a candidate on this list.

independence.statement: Top 11 takes no payment from any provider on this list. Scores are computed from a public weighted rubric; methodology weights were locked before entry research began.

changelog.text: Initial publication. Methodology v1.0 weights Automation & Segmentation (25%), Ease of Use (25%), Deliverability (20%), Pricing & Value (15%), and Integrations (15%).

answer_capsule: The best email marketing software is ActiveCampaign for its powerful automation, followed by ConvertKit for creators and Brevo for its all-in-one feature set.

criteria.name: Automation & Segmentation
criteria.description: The ability to create complex, trigger-based workflows and segment audiences based on behavior, demographics, and engagement.

criteria.name: Ease of Use & UI/UX
criteria.description: Intuitive interface, clear navigation, and a gentle learning curve for non-technical users. Includes quality of email editor and templates.

criteria.name: Deliverability & Reputation
criteria.description: The provider's technical ability and reputation to ensure emails land in the primary inbox, avoiding spam folders.

criteria.name: Pricing & Value
criteria.description: Fairness and transparency of pricing tiers relative to features offered. Generosity of free plans and scalability of cost.

criteria.name: Integrations & Ecosystem
criteria.description: Availability and quality of native integrations with other key business tools like e-commerce platforms, CRMs, and analytics.

guide.a: Are you an e-commerce store focused on abandoned carts (look at Klaviyo), a creator selling digital products (ConvertKit), or a B2B service needing CRM integration (ActiveCampaign)? Your main objective narrows the field significantly.

If you value simplicity and beautiful design above all, a tool like Flodesk or MailerLite might be best. If you're comfortable with more complex workflow builders to unlock powerful automation, ActiveCampaign or HubSpot are better fits.

Make a list of your must-have tools: your website platform (WordPress, Shopify), your CRM, your analytics tools. Ensure your chosen provider has robust, native integrations with them to avoid manual data entry and workflow headaches.

how_to_choose: Start with the free tier of your top 2-3 choices to test the user interface and email editor before committing.
Prioritize deliverability. A tool with fewer features that gets your emails opened is better than a complex one that lands in spam.
Consider your future needs. Choose a platform that can scale with your contact list and feature requirements as your business grows.

faqs.q: What is the best email marketing software for a small business?
faqs.a: For most small businesses, the best option balances ease of use, powerful automation, and affordable pricing. ActiveCampaign is a top choice for its scalability, while MailerLite offers exceptional value and simplicity for beginners.

faqs.q: Is there a good free alternative to Mailchimp?
faqs.a: Yes, several providers offer more generous free plans than Mailchimp. Brevo offers up to 300 emails/day to unlimited contacts, and MailerLite provides a free plan for up to 1,000 subscribers with solid features.

faqs.q: How much does email marketing software cost?
faqs.a: Pricing typically starts around $10-$30 per month for basic plans with up to 1,000 contacts. Costs scale with the number of subscribers and the complexity of features like automation and CRM, potentially reaching several hundred dollars per month for larger lists.

faqs.q: What's more important: deliverability or advanced features?
faqs.a: Deliverability is paramount. If your emails don't reach the inbox, even the most advanced features are useless. Always prioritize a provider with a strong reputation for high deliverability rates before evaluating their automation, segmentation, and other tools.

honest_disclosures: Most candidates are US-based, though their services are global. Pricing models can be complex, often scaling with contact list size, which can make direct comparison difficult.
This list prioritizes platforms suitable for small to medium-sized businesses; enterprise-level solutions like Salesforce Marketing Cloud were considered but not ranked.

glossary.definition: The measure of how many of your sent emails successfully reach the recipient's primary inbox, rather than being filtered into spam, promotions, or other tabs, or bouncing entirely.

entries.name: ActiveCampaign
entries.best_for: Businesses seeking the most powerful marketing automation and CRM capabilities in a single, scalable platform.
entries.verdict: ActiveCampaign is the best email marketing software for its best-in-class automation capabilities that allow for incredibly sophisticated customer journeys, combined with a built-in CRM for sales and marketing alignment.
entries.verdict_short: Unmatched automation and integrated CRM make it the top choice for businesses ready to scale their marketing efforts.
entries.praise: The visual workflow builder is both powerful and intuitive, enabling complex, multi-path automations that other platforms can't match.
entries.praise_short: Extremely powerful and flexible visual automation builder.
entries.criticism: The sheer number of features can create a steeper learning curve for absolute beginners, and its pricing can become costly at higher contact tiers.
entries.criticism_short: Steep learning curve and can get expensive.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: ConvertKit
entries.best_for: Professional creators, bloggers, and authors who need to segment their audience and sell digital products effectively.
entries.verdict: ConvertKit ranks this high because it is perfectly tailored for the creator economy, offering powerful tag-based segmentation and simple automations that make it easy to nurture an audience and sell products.
entries.verdict_short: The best platform for creators, offering powerful segmentation and tools to sell digital products with ease.
entries.praise: Its subscriber-centric model, using tags and segments instead of separate lists, is incredibly flexible and intuitive for managing a diverse audience.
entries.praise_short: Flexible tag-based system is perfect for creators.
entries.criticism: The platform's focus on creators means it lacks some of the advanced B2B features, deep e-commerce analytics, and visually complex email templates found in other tools.
entries.criticism_short: Lacks advanced B2B features and template variety.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Brevo (formerly Sendinblue)
entries.best_for: Small businesses looking for an all-in-one marketing and sales platform that includes email, SMS, chat, and more at an affordable price.
entries.verdict: Brevo earns its spot by offering an unmatched suite of tools on its free and entry-level plans, making it the best value proposition for businesses that need more than just email marketing.
entries.verdict_short: The best all-in-one platform, offering email, SMS, chat, and more with an incredibly generous free plan.
entries.praise: Its free tier is one of the most generous on the market, offering marketing automation and up to 300 emails per day to unlimited contacts.
entries.praise_short: Extremely generous free plan and feature set.
entries.criticism: While it does many things well, the depth of its features, particularly in automation and reporting, isn't as advanced as more specialized platforms like ActiveCampaign.
entries.criticism_short: Features lack the depth of specialized competitors.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: MailerLite
entries.best_for: Beginners, solopreneurs, and small businesses who prioritize ease of use, a clean interface, and excellent value for money.
entries.verdict: MailerLite stands out as the best option for users who want a simple, no-fuss email marketing tool that is incredibly easy to use and offers fantastic value, including a great free plan.
entries.verdict_short: The easiest-to-use platform on the list, offering a clean interface and excellent features for its price point.
entries.praise: Its drag-and-drop editor is exceptionally intuitive, making it possible to create beautiful emails and landing pages with no technical skill.
entries.praise_short: Superb drag-and-drop editor and user experience.
entries.criticism: Its automation and segmentation capabilities are more basic than competitors like ActiveCampaign, and the integration library is less extensive.
entries.criticism_short: Lacks advanced automation and segmentation features.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Klaviyo
entries.best_for: E-commerce businesses, particularly those on Shopify, that need deep data integration and powerful segmentation to drive sales.
entries.verdict: Klaviyo is the undisputed leader for e-commerce email marketing due to its unparalleled native integrations with platforms like Shopify, Magento, and BigCommerce, enabling hyper-targeted, revenue-driving campaigns.
entries.verdict_short: The gold standard for e-commerce, with deep data integrations that directly translate into higher store revenue.
entries.praise: The ability to pull in vast amounts of customer data from e-commerce platforms allows for incredibly precise segmentation and personalization.
entries.praise_short: Unmatched e-commerce data integration and segmentation.
entries.criticism: It is one of the more expensive options on the market, and its laser focus on e-commerce makes it less suitable for other types of businesses.
entries.criticism_short: Expensive and overly specialized for non-e-commerce.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Constant Contact
entries.best_for: Small businesses, non-profits, and event organizers who need a straightforward, reliable tool with excellent customer support.
entries.verdict: Constant Contact earns a high rank for its long-standing reputation, high deliverability rates, and user-friendly interface, making it a safe and effective choice for businesses new to email marketing.
entries.verdict_short: A reliable and easy-to-use choice for beginners, with strong deliverability and event marketing features.
entries.praise: Its event marketing tools are particularly strong, allowing for easy creation of invitations, registration forms, and follow-up campaigns.
entries.praise_short: Excellent built-in tools for event marketing.
entries.criticism: The platform feels somewhat dated compared to modern competitors, and its automation features and pricing are less competitive.
entries.criticism_short: Dated interface and less competitive on price.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: GetResponse
entries.best_for: Marketers who want an all-in-one solution that includes email marketing, landing pages, conversion funnels, and webinar hosting.
entries.verdict: GetResponse is a strong contender because it goes beyond email to offer a comprehensive marketing funnel solution, including unique features like built-in webinar hosting, making it a great value for those who use them.
entries.verdict_short: A versatile marketing suite that uniquely combines email automation with webinar and conversion funnel tools.
entries.praise: The 'Conversion Funnel' feature simplifies the process of building campaigns that guide users from ad to landing page to sale.
entries.praise_short: Integrated conversion funnel and webinar features.
entries.criticism: The user interface can feel cluttered with so many features, and the email editor, while capable, is less intuitive than top competitors.
entries.criticism_short: Cluttered interface and less polished email editor.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: HubSpot Marketing Hub
entries.best_for: Businesses already using or planning to adopt the full HubSpot ecosystem for their CRM, sales, and service needs.
entries.verdict: HubSpot's email marketing tool is powerful and seamless, but its true value is unlocked only when used as part of the broader HubSpot platform, making it the best choice for companies committed to that ecosystem.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful email tool that shines brightest when fully integrated with HubSpot's world-class CRM platform.
entries.praise: The native integration with the HubSpot CRM is flawless, providing a complete view of every contact's journey across marketing, sales, and service.
entries.praise_short: Seamless, deep integration with its own CRM.
entries.criticism: As a standalone email tool, it is significantly more expensive than competitors, and the pricing structure, with its contact tiers and add-ons, can be confusing and costly.
entries.criticism_short: Very expensive and complex pricing structure.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Drip
entries.best_for: Sophisticated e-commerce marketers who need powerful, behavior-driven automation and personalization beyond what standard tools offer.
entries.verdict: Drip is the best choice for e-commerce brands that have outgrown simpler tools and require deep personalization, offering powerful 'if-this-then-that' logic and visual workflows tailored for online stores.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful, e-commerce-first platform for brands needing highly sophisticated automation and personalization.
entries.praise: Its visual workflow builder and rule-based triggers allow for extremely granular segmentation and personalized customer journeys based on shopping behavior.
entries.criticism: Drip is more complex and expensive than many alternatives, making it overkill for businesses outside of e-commerce or those just starting out.
entries.criticism_short: Complex and expensive for non-e-commerce users.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Flodesk
entries.best_for: Solopreneurs, creatives, and small businesses who prioritize beautiful email design and simplicity over complex automation.
entries.verdict: Flodesk makes the list because it offers the most aesthetically pleasing templates and the simplest user experience on the market, making it perfect for brands where visual identity is paramount.
entries.verdict_short: The best choice for creating visually stunning emails with an incredibly simple interface and flat-rate pricing.
entries.praise: Its template library and email editor are design-forward, allowing users to create gorgeous, on-brand emails without any design skills.
entries.criticism: It significantly lags behind competitors in advanced features like segmentation, A/B testing, and native integrations, making it unsuitable for data-driven marketers.
entries.criticism_short: Lacks advanced marketing and analytics features.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Beehiiv
entries.best_for: Modern newsletter creators and publishers focused on growth, monetization, and community engagement.
entries.verdict: Beehiiv is our wildcard pick because it represents the future of newsletter-first media, blending a powerful email service with built-in tools for monetization, referrals, and website hosting, challenging the traditional ESP model.
entries.verdict_short: A newsletter-centric platform with built-in growth and monetization tools, representing a new breed of ESP.
entries.praise: It offers a suite of tools specifically designed for newsletter growth, including a referral program, paid subscription options, and ad network access.
entries.praise_short: Excellent built-in newsletter growth and monetization tools.
entries.criticism: Its marketing automation and segmentation features are currently more basic than traditional ESPs like ActiveCampaign, as its focus is on publishing rather than complex customer journeys.

risk_signals.summary: As a relatively new, venture-backed company in a competitive space, there is a higher degree of product roadmap and pricing uncertainty compared to established players.
signals: Founded in 2021, limited long-term track record.
